description: Reference design language for compact native desktop utilities using restrained acrylic surfaces, dense picker rows, and no component-level blur stacking.
rules: Use native OS acrylic/vibrancy for blur; do not add CSS backdrop-filter to controls.
Prefer dense rows over dashboard cards for task-completion utilities.
Keep one primary action; secondary and tertiary actions must remain visually quieter.
Do not use hero headers, marketing copy, glowing buttons, neon accents, or decorative glass borders.
Use compositing math when matching tones across layered translucent surfaces.
